article.title: Qualcomm in Talks to Buy Jim Keller's RISC-V AI Chip Maker Tenstorrent for $8 Billion to $10 Billion
article.summary: Reports say Qualcomm is examining a full acquisition of Tenstorrent, the RISC-V AI accelerator startup led by Jim Keller, at a valuation between $8 billion and $10 billion.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

Qualcomm is in talks to acquire Tenstorrent, the AI accelerator startup led by veteran chip architect Jim Keller, in a deal that would value the company between $8 billion and $10 billion, according to [Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/technology/ai/articles/qualcomm-talks-acquire-ai-chip-230401789.html), which carried a Reuters report. The valuation range was first reported by The Information and picked up by Reuters on June 15, 2026, according to [igor'sLAB](https://www.igorslab.de/en/?p=322217). If completed, the deal would deepen Qualcomm's push beyond its smartphone roots and into the data-center AI market.

## What We Know

The talks center on a complete acquisition of Tenstorrent, a company founded in 2016 and led by Jim Keller, a former Apple chip designer who also oversaw Tesla's efforts to develop a chip for autonomous driving, according to [Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/technology/ai/articles/qualcomm-talks-acquire-ai-chip-230401789.html). Keller's earlier work spans "DEC Alpha, AMD K7 and K8, the Zen family, Apple's A4 and A5, and Tesla's first in-house chip for automated driving," and he was also Senior Vice President of the Silicon Engineering Group at Intel, according to [igor'sLAB](https://www.igorslab.de/en/?p=322217).

Tenstorrent builds scalable AI accelerators for both the cloud and edge, according to [Data Center Dynamics](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/qualcomm-considers-acquiring-ai-chip-firm-tenstorrent/). The company develops complete computer systems for AI that combine Tensix compute cores, AI accelerators, RISC-V processor IP, systems, and compiler technologies, according to [igor'sLAB](https://www.igorslab.de/en/?p=322217). Its chips are based on RISC-V, an open-source CPU design approach, and the company emphasizes that they can handle certain AI workloads more efficiently than Nvidia GPUs, according to [Digital Today](https://www.digitaltoday.co.kr/en/view/63718/qualcomm-in-talks-to-buy-jim-keller-ai-chip-startup-tenstorrent).

The discussions are ongoing, and the deal value could change, with the possibility that negotiations could still fall through, according to [Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/technology/ai/articles/qualcomm-talks-acquire-ai-chip-230401789.html). Both companies declined to comment, with Tenstorrent declining to comment and Qualcomm saying it does not comment on rumors, according to [Data Center Dynamics](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/qualcomm-considers-acquiring-ai-chip-firm-tenstorrent/).

## The Valuation Backdrop

A deal at $8 billion to $10 billion would mark a steep markup from Tenstorrent's recent funding. The company closed a financing round at the end of 2024 for more than $693 million at a valuation of $2 billion before the new financing, according to [igor'sLAB](https://www.igorslab.de/en/?p=322217). In 2025, it was in talks to raise $800 million at a $3.2 billion valuation, according to [Data Center Dynamics](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/qualcomm-considers-acquiring-ai-chip-firm-tenstorrent/).

Tenstorrent has raised more than $1 billion from Fidelity, AFW Partners and Eclipse Ventures, among others, with Hyundai and Kia also investors and partners, according to [Digital Today](https://www.digitaltoday.co.kr/en/view/63718/qualcomm-in-talks-to-buy-jim-keller-ai-chip-startup-tenstorrent). Its December 2024 round drew investors including Jeff Bezos' Bezos Expeditions, Samsung Securities and AFW Partner, according to [Data Center Dynamics](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/qualcomm-considers-acquiring-ai-chip-firm-tenstorrent/).

## Qualcomm's Diversification Push

Qualcomm derives a large share of its revenue from smartphone and PC chips and is looking for ways to expand its stake in the AI chip market, according to [Digital Today](https://www.digitaltoday.co.kr/en/view/63718/qualcomm-in-talks-to-buy-jim-keller-ai-chip-startup-tenstorrent). Last year the company acquired Alphawave Semi for $2.4 billion, according to [Digital Today](https://www.digitaltoday.co.kr/en/view/63718/qualcomm-in-talks-to-buy-jim-keller-ai-chip-startup-tenstorrent). Qualcomm chief executive Cristiano Amon has framed the data-center market as "a new growth opportunity for Qualcomm and is a logical extension of our diversification strategy," a comment he made during Qualcomm's fiscal Q3 2025 earnings, according to [Data Center Dynamics](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/qualcomm-considers-acquiring-ai-chip-firm-tenstorrent/).

The reported Tenstorrent talks follow Qualcomm's separately announced agreement on June 24 to acquire AI software startup Modular in an all-stock deal worth about $3.9 billion, as [previously reported](/article/2026-06/25-qualcomm-to-buy-ai-software-startup-modular-for-about-39-billion-taking-aim-at-nvidias-cuda-moat). Together, the moves point to a strategy that pairs hardware and software aimed at the AI infrastructure stack dominated by Nvidia.

## What We Don't Know

Neither side has confirmed the talks, and Reuters was unable to independently verify the report, according to [Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/technology/ai/articles/qualcomm-talks-acquire-ai-chip-230401789.html). It also remains unclear whether the proposed price includes performance-based milestone payments, a structure that has been used in previous acquisitions of chip startups, according to [Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/technology/ai/articles/qualcomm-talks-acquire-ai-chip-230401789.html). No closing timeline or regulatory pathway for a potential Tenstorrent deal has been disclosed.
